summary: Completed comprehensive testing of TripSync AI-powered travel planning platform. Backend APIs working perfectly (18/18 tests passed), but frontend protected routes stuck in loading state due to authentication refresh token issues.

ui_bugs.issue: Protected routes (dashboard, create-trip, admin) stuck in 'Loading TripSync...' state
ui_bugs.selector: All protected pages show loading spinner indefinitely

integration_issues.issue: Continuous 401 errors from /api/auth/refresh endpoint causing infinite loading loop
integration_issues.details: Frontend AuthContext continuously attempts token refresh but receives 401 responses, preventing protected pages from rendering

passed_tests: Landing page loads with hero, how-it-works, trip types, destinations sections
Auth page loads correctly with login/register forms
Admin login successful with correct credentials (admin@tripsync.com/admin123)
Navigation between landing and auth pages works
Backend API: POST /api/auth/register creates new user
Backend API: POST /api/auth/login works with admin credentials
Backend API: GET /api/auth/me returns user data
Backend API: POST /api/trips creates trip with invite code
Backend API: GET /api/trips/{id}/recommendations returns scored destinations
Backend API: Voting system (POST/GET votes) working
Backend API: Comments system (POST/GET comments) working
Backend API: AI summary generation with GPT-5.2 working
All 18 backend API tests passed with 100% success rate
action_item_for_main_agent: Fix frontend authentication refresh token mechanism. The AuthContext is making continuous failed requests to /api/auth/refresh (401 errors) which keeps protected routes in loading state. Need to debug token storage/retrieval and refresh logic in AuthContext.
